name: REFLUX NEPHROPATHY
definition: CHRONIC ATROPHIC PYELONEPHRITIS ascending bacterial urinary tract infection ← reflux of infected urine from lower tract +

demographics.Age: usually young adults (subclinical diagnosis starting in childhood); M < F

dominant_finding: focally increased echogenicity within cortex ← scar
distribution: normal / small kidney; uni- / bilateral; uni- / multifocal
discriminating_features: normal / small kidney; uni- / bilateral; uni- / multifocal
focal parenchymal thinning with contour depression in upper / lower pole (more compound
retracted papilla with clubbed calyx subjacent to scar
contralateral / focal compensatory hypertrophy (= renal pseudotumor)
dilated ureters ← reflux, sometimes with linear striations (= redundant / edematous mucosa)

general: normal / small kidney; uni- / bilateral; uni- / multifocal
focal parenchymal thinning with contour depression in upper / lower pole (more compound
retracted papilla with clubbed calyx subjacent to scar
contralateral / focal compensatory hypertrophy (= renal pseudotumor)
dilated ureters ← reflux, sometimes with linear striations (= redundant / edematous mucosa)
US: focally increased echogenicity within cortex ← scar
ANGIO: small tortuous intrarenal arteries, pruning of intrarenal vessels
